title: [Feature]: Strip newlines when parsing kubernetes pod yaml hostPath paths
body:
### Feature request description

I made a mistake in the kubernetes pod yaml file. To format it nicely I wrapped the `hostPath` in named volumes using the `>` yaml symbol:

```yaml
...
  volumes:
    - name: foo
      hostPath:
        path: >  # note the misplaced > character here
          /very/long/long/long/long/long/path
        type: Directory
```

instead of

```yaml
...
  volumes:
    - name: foo
      hostPath:
        path:  # no > character
          /very/long/long/long/long/long/path
        type: Directory
```

which results in the path `/very/long/long/long/long/long/path\n` (final newline!) instead of `/very/long/long/long/long/long/path` after parsing the yaml. Podman complained about the non existent path (no such file or directory) but in the error message the `\n` was truncated, which made it very difficult to debug the issue.

### Suggest potential solution

Either truncate the final newline directly after parsing or show the final new line also in the error message.

### Have you considered any alternatives?

I could easily fix the issue after fining it by just remove the misplaced `>` symbol which I thought I need.

### Additional context

_No response_

comments.body: Can you show the full error message? We cannot strip newline because it can be a valid file path but I agree the error should must it.

Hi @Luap99,

sorry, my fault. The error message is indeed correct:

```
Error: failed to create volume "wpconfig": in parsing HostPath in YAML: stat /very/long/long/long/long/long/path
: no such file or directory
```

However I did not recognize the newline after the path. I'm not sure, if it is worth it to highlight special characters.
